heading: Prohibition on use of “Rule of 78’s” in connection with mortgage refinancings and other consumer loans
label: 1615 Prohibition on use of “Rule of 78’s” in connection with mortgage refinancings and other consumer loans
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992, and not as part of the Consumer Credit Protection Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Prompt refund of unearned interest required
children.label: a Prompt refund of unearned interest required

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If a consumer prepays in full the financed amount under any consumer credit transaction, the creditor shall promptly refund any unearned portion of the interest charge to the consumer.

children.heading: Exception for refund of de minimus amount1So in original. Probably should be “de minimis”.1
children.label: 2 Exception for refund of de minimus amount1So in original. Probably should be “de minimis”.1
children.text: No refund shall be required under paragraph (1) with respect to the prepayment of any consumer credit transaction if the total amount of the refund would be less than $1.

children.heading: Applicability to refinanced transactions and acceleration by the creditor
children.label: 3 Applicability to refinanced transactions and acceleration by the creditor
A
children.text: any prepayment made in connection with the refinancing, consolidation, or restructuring of the transaction; and

children.label: B
children.text: any prepayment made as a result of the acceleration of the obligation to repay the amount due with respect to the transaction.

children.heading: Use of “Rule of 78’s” prohibited
children.label: b Use of “Rule of 78’s” prohibited
children.text: For the purpose of calculating any refund of interest required under subsection (a) for any precomputed consumer credit transaction of a term exceeding 61 months which is consummated after , the creditor shall compute the refund based on a method which is at least as favorable to the consumer as the actuarial method.September 30, 1993

children.label: A
children.text: the amount necessary to prepay the account in full; and

children.label: B
children.text: if the amount disclosed pursuant to subparagraph (A) includes an amount which is required to be refunded under this section with respect to such prepayment, the amount of such refund.

children.heading: Written statement required if request is in writing
children.label: 2 Written statement required if request is in writing
children.text: If the customer’s request is in writing, the statement under paragraph (1) shall be in writing.

children.heading: 1 free annual statement
children.label: 3 1 free annual statement
children.text: A consumer shall be entitled to obtain 1 statement under paragraph (1) each year without charge.

children.heading: Additional statements subject to reasonable fees
children.label: 4 Additional statements subject to reasonable fees
children.text: Any creditor may impose a reasonable fee to cover the cost of providing any statement under paragraph (1) to any consumer in addition to the 1 free annual statement required under paragraph (3) if the amount of the charge for such additional statement is disclosed to the consumer before furnishing such statement.

children.heading: Actuarial method
children.label: 1 Actuarial method
children.text: The term “actuarial method” means the method of allocating payments made on a debt between the amount financed and the finance charge pursuant to which a payment is applied first to the accumulated finance charge and any remainder is subtracted from, or any deficiency is added to, the unpaid balance of the amount financed.

children.heading: Consumer, credit
children.label: 2 Consumer, credit
children.text: The terms “consumer” and “creditor” have the meanings given to such terms in .section 1602 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: has the meaning given to such term in ; andsection 1602 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: includes any assignee of any creditor with respect to credit extended in connection with any consumer credit transaction and any subsequent assignee with respect to such credit.
